records.ety: [OE. schowen, shewen, schewen, shawen, AS. scewian, to look, see, view; akin to OS. scaw<?/n, OFries. skawia, D. schouwen, OHG. scouw<?/n, G. schauen, Dan. skue, Sw. sk<?/da, Icel. sko<?/a, Goth. usskawjan to waken, skuggwa a mirror, Icel. skuggy shade, shadow, L. cavere to be on one's guard, Gr. <?/<?/<?/ to mark, perceive, hear, Skr. kavi wise. Cf. Caution, Scavenger, Sheen.]
records.morph: [imp. Showed (?); p. p. Shown (?) or Showed; p. pr. & vb. n. Showing. It is sometimes written shew, shewed, shewn, shewing.]

defs: To exhibit or present to view; to place in sight; to display; -- the thing exhibited being the object, and often with an indirect object denoting the person or thing seeing or beholding; as, to show a house; show your colors; shopkeepers show customers goods (show goods to customers).
quotes: Go thy way, shew thyself to the priest.
Nor want we skill or art from whence to raise Magnificence; and what can heaven show more?

defs: To exhibit to the mental view; to tell; to disclose; to reveal; to make known; as, to show one's designs.
quotes: Shew them the way wherein they must walk.
If it please my father to do thee evil, then I will shew it thee, and send thee away.

defs: Specifically, to make known the way to (a person); hence, to direct; to guide; to usher; to conduct; as, to show a person into a parlor; to show one to the door.
To make apparent or clear, as by evidence, testimony, or reasoning; to prove; to explain; also, to manifest; to evince; as, to show the truth of a statement; to show the causes of an event.
quotes: I 'll show my duty by my timely care.

defs: To bestow; to confer; to afford; as, to show favor.
quotes: Shewing mercy unto thousands of them that love me.

defs: To exhibit or manifest one's self or itself; to appear; to look; to be in appearance; to seem.
quotes: Just such she shows before a rising storm.
All round a hedge upshoots, and shows At distance like a little wood.

defs: To have a certain appearance, as well or ill, fit or unfit; to become or suit; to appear.
quotes: My lord of York, it better showed with you.

defs: The act of showing, or bringing to view; exposure to sight; exhibition.
That which is shown, or brought to view; that which is arranged to be seen; a spectacle; an exhibition; as, a traveling show; a cattle show.
quotes: As for triumphs, masks, feasts, and such shows.

defs: Proud or ostentatious display; parade; pomp.
quotes: I envy none their pageantry and show.
He through the midst unmarked, In show plebeian angel militant Of lowest order, passed.
Beware of the scribes, . . . which devour widows' houses, and for a shew make long prayers.

defs: A discharge, from the vagina, of mucus streaked with blood, occurring a short time before labor.
A pale blue flame, at the top of a candle flame, indicating the presence of fire damp.
